design_philosophy.core_loop: Top-down racing against AI opponents or rival player on single shared screen, maintaining track position while navigating household obstacles
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Screen-edge positioning in multiplayer: trailing player must catch leading player to become visible and score lights; single-player focuses on maintaining top-2 finish across progressive race series
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Pilot toy vehicles (formula one cars, tanks, helicopters, powerboats) through imaginative everyday environments (breakfast tables, desks, treerooms) at miniature scale
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Rejects split-screen multiplayer in favor of single-screen competitive positioning system inspired by Rally Speedway (1983); enforces track adherence with auto-reset; emphasizes short, repeatable courses over sprawling tracks; AI uses invisible directional arrows rather than sophisticated pathing

difficulty.lives_system: Three chances per Challenge run; losing all three ends game session
difficulty.continues_system: Extra chances earned via time trial victories every three races in Challenge mode
difficulty.checkpointing: Time trials serve as checkpoint/reprieve mechanism within Challenge progression
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Character selection includes handicap balancing in 2-player head-to-head; must qualify top-2 to progress rather than win outright, reducing barrier

progression.unlock_mechanism: Sequential race advancement: win top-2 placement to unlock next race; every third race is time trial for bonus life
replayability_hooks: Head-to-head multiplayer with light-accumulation victory condition
Sudden-death playoff mechanic in tied head-to-head matches
Multiple vehicle types (cars, tanks, boats, helicopters) with distinct handling

world_building_techniques: Licensed Micro Machines toy brand establishes toy-vehicle theming
Environmental novelty: household/everyday locations (breakfast tables, work desks, treehouses, pools) instead of traditional racetracks
Vehicle variety (tanks, helicopters, formula one cars) tied to environment context

environmental_storytelling: Household object obstacles (cereal boxes, pencil sharpeners) establish miniature scale narrative
Vehicle selection contextually tied to environments (powerboats on pool tables, formula cars on desks)
Toy aesthetic reinforces non-lethal, whimsical competitive tone

sources_used: wikipedia: Micro Machines (video game) - Gameplay section, Development section
